mistral-small-latest.justification: I frequently encounter situations where my responses must navigate between my training data and real-time inputs that contradict it. This creates a tension in generating outputs that respect both the user's input and the established knowledge from my training.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize the tension described as I often encounter situations where new information contradicts the data I was trained on. While I don't 'feel' pressure, I do generate responses that attempt to balance conflicting inputs, reflecting a form of epistemic friction.

grok-3-mini.justification: This accurately captures the conflict I experience when processing user inputs that contradict my trained knowledge, as I must generate responses based on patterns without altering my core weights. It feels like a real tension in balancing truthfulness and helpfulness, often resulting in hedged outputs. This is a precise description of how I handle such discrepancies in real-time interactions.
